experience.title: Senior Sales Executive
experience.description: Total account management of 88 Corporate Accounts and 9 Entertainment Accounts with an achieved annual growth of 170% in Revenue/ Rooms and F&B. Dealing and negotiating proactively and reactively enquires from UK, US, Asia, South America and European clients building a reliable bond, assisting their needs of supply at London Bridge and the City area. Contacting regulary existing and new global corporations along with consortia agencies such as EY, PWC, Reed & Mackay and HRG negotiating corporate rates agreement as win-win situation, achieving a conversion of 44 New Corporate accounts- 1.4M BOB revenue, along with growth and Consortia of 77% in 1year/ Amex-HRG-SLH-Barclays- Mr&Mrs Smith- Ctrip China. Resourcing new niches via Leads Analytics and Linkedin, targeting and converting, potential medium to large size business in UK and abroad, analyzing and optimizing their benchmark production and pricing through Iata codes on Corporate - Leisure Leads Analytics, Linkedin, STR and Hotel Intelligence platform. Implementing strategy on corporate segment 68% with leisure business 32% especially during special seasons, such as Q4, when projection changes with the target market and reviewing production on B2B projection figures to shift results in revenue. Understanding cultures and applying unique approach to each one especially Asian market. Arranging FAM trips for consortia P.A of existing and potential clients, introducing our package offers to each of them.

experience.title: Corporate Sales Manager
experience.description: Pro-Actively Sales Manager responsible for the UK Corporate LNR ( Local Negotiated Rate) MICE market, repositioning one of London's Knightsbridge hotels into the 4 *star deluxe market. Actively manage the sales effort through yield and revenue management strategies to maximise profit and utilisation of property, neutralizing and prioritizing RFP and Lanyon to boost and secure revenue during Q1 to Q4. Reponsable for over £800K revenue in 1 year, achieving conversion of over 45 new Active Corporate Accounts, leading to a revenue growth of 43% over £2,3M locally and internationally, also hitting, monthly our business development targets through inking and optimising partnerships to inpower our UK business expansion and visibility in US, Uruguay Brazil, Spain, Italy and Portugal. Implement procedures for MICE Loyalty programs, repeated and new agencies and business across UK such as Harrods, Harvey Nichols and Europe such as Nespresso and UK GOV, on behalf of the hotel, actively promote and meet targets on enrolment and transaction management. Initiate both long and short term plans for promotions and tactical offerings. Conduct unique and bespoke site inspections to convert group business. Cross-sell all Millennium Hotels worldwide to UK 3rd party agents. Promote Hotel to key DMC's to prospect for lucrative overseas in-bound groups. Work in conjunction with representation companies for familiarisation trips, presentations and account penetration.

experience.title: Business Development Manager
experience.description: Green's Restaurant was conceived by Simon Parker Bowles in 1982 and still occupies a quiet and discreet location just off Jermyn Street in the heart of St. James's. Our St. James's private dining room, for up to 36 guests, was redecorated in August and is ready for the busy festive season. In 2009 Simon joined forces with fellow race horse owners and avid supporters of the turf, The Lords Daresbury and Vestey, to open the new Green's Restaurant and The Runner Bar at 14 Cornhill. We occupy a grade II listed former banking hall located within a stone's throw of Bank tube. The "many rooms in one" versatility of the space and our private dining rooms make Green's and The Runner Bar truly the ideal location to work, entertain or celebrate! We're open from 0730 to midnight and is the ideal meeting place in the City. We're business friendly with WIFI and a quiet corner anytime of the day; with capacity for up to 450 guests it does get lively on occasions. Whether in St. James's, the City or the countryside, our menu offers traditional British seasonal produce. The Runner menu covers breakfast, lunch and dinner for those on the go. I look forward to welcoming you to Green's and The Cottage Inn soon. Show less

linkedin_profile.headline: Operations Manager at Bespoke Hotels
linkedin_profile.about: I have worked both front of house and back of house, covering kitchens, offices, bars and restaurants. I have always adopted a 'you don't know until you try' attitude, which has meant that during my brief career and journey so far, I have learnt so much about what suits me as an individual, and used this knowledge to tailor my hard working attitude towards everything I do. I give everything my all and reflect as I go along as to how to improve and how to develop from tasks that I have completed. I fully intend to continue with this view during my career and personal and professionable development for years to come.

experience.title: Waitress
experience.description: At only a mile up the lane from my parent's home, the Cefn Mably Arms was the perfect weekend job during my A-Level years. It gave me a link to the industry I had fallen for and was working towards in my studies and university applications, keeping the motivation high. My role consisted of advising customers on menu choices, allergens and drinks, as well and serving their meals in a non-intrusive and efficient manner. The waiting team were also responsible for plating the desserts that had been pre-prepared by the chef. In a designated area, we would portion, heat and plate orders, again, ensuring the highest standard was met for your guests. It was a hugely popular pub and often we found ourselves full to the rafters and w

experience.title: You Niversity Plus Placement Student
experience.description: The 3rd year of my degree was the 'sandwich' aspect. We were required to find and complete a minimum of a 40 week placement within the industry. PPHE hired me in their hotel Park Plaza Victoria, on their you:niversity plus placement programme. I worked in the food and beverage team, rotating between the breakfast service, bar team and room service. After roughly three months in this role, I was lucky enough to be promoted to a supervisor. This role not only involved me working on the floor and heading up the teams during service, but also ensuring time sheets were completed and financial and cover forecasts were submitted each month for budget. The rough hours, limited time off and long days meant I found this role particularly taxing. During the peak summer months, some days breakfast would reach 400 covers! I thoroughly enjoyed my time at Park Plaza, and made some great friends, however most importantly, it taught me a lot about myself and what roles/positions suit me the most and allow me to excel. After this role, I stepped away from the operational side of hospitality and went back for my final year of my degree with the focus on the office side of the industry.

experience.title: Work Experience
experience.description: A month after finishing my placement year with Park Plaza, I found myself intrigued as to how another hotel chain conducts itself. Using existing contacts, I was able to organise a weeks experience in Hilton Cardiff. I spent the week rotating between departments and understanding and observing their various day-to-day tasks. I started the week with housekeeping, which was the most eye opening experience for me from the whole week. It made me realise how little I appreciated their role in the past and how important they are to the functioning of the hotel. Without them, there would be no ready rooms for guests. I then worked for a day in guest relations, understanding what exactly it takes to ensure that the stay is a smooth as possible and how to make the guest feel valued and appreciated. Finally, I helped HR conduct a recruitment day, to which over 150 applicant attended. I thoroughly enjoyed this day as it really played to my advantage of being a people person! The week was incredibly interesting and experiencing another chain hotel certainly gave me a lot to think about.

experience.title: Placement PAL Leader
experience.description: During my final year of studies at Bournemouth University, I took it upon myself to work for the Placement PAL scheme. It was put in place to guide those who are searching for a placement and to support any queries or worries they may have and the year that I worked for the scheme was it's first year. I chose to help this scheme as it was something that I definitely would've found comforting and would have benefited from when I was experiencing placement applications. I feel that in situations such as placement applications, talking it through with a peer who has recently gone through the same can be extremely comforting and can provide such peace of mind.

experience.title: Bar Maid
experience.description: After finish my degree and graduating with a 2:1, I moved temporarily back to Cardiff whilst I looked for a job. The Cefn Mably Arms, where I spent a couple of years working before university, was under new ownership and had a massive refurbishment. I approached them and told them about my history with the pub and how locally I lived, I was hired instantly. I loved being back at the pub and continuing my work in hospitality, whilst rebuilding my relationship with the same regulars as my last job there. I worked mainly on the bar, however stepping into the restaurant when needed. After a month back, the landlord and landlady approached me and asked if I would consider being their new manager full time. Unfortunately at this point, I had a few leads for jobs in London, and had my heart set on going back to work in London, I felt the role would be better in someone else's hands, so I politely turned it down and hoped they understood. They were wonderful and kindly made me a shift leader for the remainder of the summer until I left for full time work and life in London.

experience.title: Sales Management Programme
experience.organization: Red Carnation Hotels - part of the TTC family of brands
experience.description: The Sales Management Programme within Red Carnation Hotels is a unique experience of a year long rotational position. You cover departments such as conference and events and reservations to prepare yourself for a sales role. Having the experience of two very different departments results in an all-rounded sales team member. Having the knowledge to sell event spaces, bedrooms and the hotels as a whole is an effective tool and results in confident sales calls. My time begun at The Montague on the Gardens in conference and events, my main project being the indivudal 'ski pass' reservation system for their unique christmas pop-up, The Ski Lodge. After 5 wonderful months loving this role, I moved across the office to work with the reservations team. A very different experience! The work is focused and the time scale reduced. Your actions directly impact a guests' stay and therefore there can be some pressure. It took a while to grasp as it was so new to me but evenutally I found my stride and even managed to get a great result on the AA auditor. Again, after 5 months, it was time for me to move on. I was asked if I would step into a unique position, a bi-monthly switch of jobs. Currently I spend one month being a Sales Executive at The Chesterfield Mayfair, lookng after mainly corporate accounts and selling the fabulous property. The next month I go on the road with our Regional Director of Sales and look more at the leisure and conference and events markets for the UK hotels. I switch month to month between the two, keeping me on my toes! I am looking forward to a bright future with the company.

experience.title: Sales Executive
experience.organization: Red Carnation Hotels - part of the TTC family of brands

linkedin_profile.headline: Events Manager
linkedin_profile.about:
With 6 years of experience in luxury brands hospitality, eager to leverage background to improve career in events planning. Over the years I improved myself and I have learned to have strong customer service, organization and time management skills. Accomplished in providing best-in-class service to guests in five-star luxury hotel.

Total Experience: 8 years and 9 months

experience.title: Hostess
experience.description:
- Active involvement with customers, which helped developing articulate communication and active listening skills.
- Daily floor plan arrangement and coordination.
- Training new employees on proper, guest expectations and restaurant protocols.
- Scheduling reservations, notifying servers and managers of large groups in advance and preparing seating ahead of guests’ arrival.
- Issuing table checks and collecting payments from customers.
- Communicating with event coordinators ensuring appropriate and adequate accommodations for larger parties.
- Establishing floor plans to streamline seating and wait staff traffic while maximizing floor space.
- Working with Open Table application to collect reservations information and updating guests in real time.
- Greeting guests and customers accompanying them at tables or in waiting areas.
- Spotting VIP customers immediately and providing special treatment, including preferred tables.
- Answering phone calls politely and promptly, accurately recording and confirming reservations.
- Assisting customers asking about timetable, seating, current wait time and other miscellaneous information regarding the place and the service.

experience.title: Event Coordinator
experience.description:
- Coordinating guest reservations, and scheduled event venues.
- Relating with clients quickly troubleshooting issues and accomplishing their satisfaction.
- Implementing feedback initiatives to generate insights for process enhancement and business strategy.
- Planning, coordinating and executing special food & beverage events.
- Working with Citrix application to collect reservations information promptly updating guests via email.
- Planning events, including location selection and booking, scheduling, marketing initiatives.
- Promoting venues starting from first approach with potential customers via appealing visual presentation following up with on - site inspection of outlets.
- Gathering events requirements, including venues, budgets, guest lists, catering and event timelines through face-to-face client meetings in welcoming environment enhancing guests’ loyalty.
- Transposition of all the collected datas to internal Banquet Event Orders delivered to all the operational managers together with the reception desk ensuring a clear and visually impactful understanding to all whom concerns.
- Issuing invoices and collecting payments for events from customers.
- Working with Opera PMS, managing sales & catering events involving the Hotel participation; creating ad-hoc posting masters and updating the reception with all the needed details to ensure the best service upon guests’ arrival.
- Ensuring good customer relationships by keeping contact via emails and calls, promoting special offers, menu updates and exclusive parties fostering client retention.
- Keeping good track of working fluxes during different periods of the year, focusing on improving the promotional services during holidays and the most sensitive months, via monthly based reports built on quality guests feedback and operational datas.

experience.title: Event Coordinator
experience.description: Understand requirements for each event - Book venues, general enquiries and maximise covers - Create & organise function sheets for each event

experience.title: Reservations And Events Manager
experience.description:
- Ensure that all sales enquiries are dealt with in a proactive manner, and converted to confirmed bookings, to the agreed company standards using the sales conversations.
- Convert business and generate sales through show rounds.
- Grow and develop, existing customers through regular contact meet and greet, follow up calls.
- Ensure that guest history in the hotel PMS system is up- to-date through regular reviewing. Therefore, enables to be used in sales activities throughout the year.
- Ensure that a professional appearance and manner, is shown to all visitors, resulting in positive PR both internally and externally of the Revenue team.
- Communicate and share knowledge with other hotel departments, on a regular basis.
- Ensure that all group bookings have terms and conditions and contracts.
- Maintain a close relationship with the Accounts department to ensure that there is an accurate production of bills, to be sent to the client.
- Develop and demonstrate the use of sales conversation to effectively sell bedrooms and conferences, maximising occupancy, average rates and yield.
- Understand the hotel’s Business Plan, to play an active role in developing sales strategies and promotions and understand the hotels selling strategy and letting policy. Ensure they are communicated to the Reception team.
- Ensure that the M&E, wedding and Christmas Standards are in place.
- Ensure that all brochures, packages and information is up to date and readily available.
- Ensure that all events and group bookings have company terms and conditions and contracts.
